info.name: Expense Tracker API
info.description:
REST API collection for the Expense Tracker backend (Gin + SQLite).

Base path: `/api`. All dates are ISO 8601 in UTC. Server-generated fields:
`id`, `createdAt`, `updatedAt`. All resource bodies also include `userId`
(the owner).

**Auth:** stateful sessions. Server stores sessions in the `sessions` table;
the client receives a `session_id` cookie (`HttpOnly`, `Secure` (config),
`SameSite=Lax`) with a 24h TTL (sliding). Postman stores the cookie in its
cookie jar automatically — no Authorization header is needed. Run `Register`
or `Login` first; subsequent requests reuse the cookie.

**Money:** all monetary fields (`amount`, `openingBalance`, `manualAdjustment`,
`balance`, `netWorth`) are `integer` (int64) in **minor units** (cents for
USD/EUR/RUB). $12.50 → `1250`. No fractional part on the wire.

**Rate limits:** `Login` — 5 failed attempts / 5 min per IP → 429.
`Register` — 10 / hour per IP → 429. Other endpoints — no limit yet.

Run `Create Account`, `Create Category`, `Create Income Transaction` first —
their `Tests` scripts capture the generated IDs into the active environment
(`accountId`, `categoryId`, `transactionId`), so subsequent GET/PATCH/DELETE
requests just work.

item.name: Auth
item.description:
Stateful session auth. `Register` and `Login` create a session and set the
`session_id` cookie. `Logout` deletes the session and clears the cookie
(idempotent). `Me` returns the current user.

Rate-limited: 10 register/hour/IP, 5 failed login/5 min/IP.

response.name: 201 Created — user + session cookie + 24 seed categories
400 VALIDATION_FAILED — invalid email / short password
429 TOO_MANY_REQUESTS — 5 failed attempts in 5 min
204 No Content — session deleted, cookie cleared
401 UNAUTHORIZED — no/invalid cookie or expired session

item.name: Accounts
item.description:
CRUD for accounts + balance summary. All endpoints require auth; a user
sees only their own accounts.

response.name: 400 VALIDATION_FAILED — missing required fields
400 VALIDATION_FAILED — name too short (min=3)

item.name: Categories
item.description:
CRUD for categories. Categories are per-user: on `Register`, 24 default
categories are copied into the new user's account. After that the user owns
them and can edit or delete any of them, and add new ones.

`name` is unique per `(userId, name)`.

response.name: 200 OK — expense categories (mix of seeded + custom)

item.name: Transactions
item.description:
CRUD for transactions of 3 types: `income`, `expense`, `transfer`.

Shape rules (400 VALIDATION_FAILED):
- cashflow (income/expense): `accountId`, `categoryId` required; `fromAccountId`, `toAccountId` forbidden.
- transfer: `fromAccountId`, `toAccountId` required; `accountId`, `categoryId` forbidden.

Referential integrity (422): non-existent account/category, type mismatch between transaction and category, same-account transfer.

`type` is immutable via PATCH.

query.description: Optional. For transfer, matches both fromAccountId and toAccountId.
Optional. ISO date, inclusive. Must be >= fromDate.

response.name: 400 VALIDATION_FAILED — toDate before fromDate
400 VALIDATION_FAILED — cashflow shape rule broken (missing accountId/categoryId)
422 CATEGORY_TYPE_MISMATCH — expense tx with income category
400 VALIDATION_FAILED — transfer shape rule broken (missing toAccountId)
422 SAME_ACCOUNT_TRANSFER — from and to are identical
400 VALIDATION_FAILED — cashflow tx cannot accept fromAccountId
